consumer_purpose: Address 3 P0 plugin security gaps: unscoped SDK client, unscoped shell ($), and unrestricted process.env/fetch() access. Must be backwards compatible and leverage existing CapabilityId/TrustLevel infrastructure.

root_causes.hypothesis: PluginInput is constructed once and shared across all plugins — no per-plugin scoping exists at the SDK/shell level
evidence: packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts:218-239 — client and PluginInput constructed once before any plugin loads
packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts:226 — same input object passed to all applyPlugin calls
packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts:239 — $: typeof Bun === 'undefined' ? undefined : Bun.$ — raw BunShell, no gating
packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts:167-179 — applyPlugin receives input but doesn't scope it per-plugin

root_causes.hypothesis: HookDispatchGuard only gates hook dispatch — SDK calls, shell access, and globals are completely unguarded
evidence: packages/opencode/src/plugin/capability/hook-dispatch-guard.ts:40-63 — shouldDispatch only checks hook names against HOOK_CAPABILITY_MAP
packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts:437-445 — trigger() uses dispatchGuard.shouldDispatch() for hooks only, never for SDK calls
packages/opencode/src/plugin/capability/enforcer.ts:20-31 — checkCapability exists but is only called from HookDispatchGuard and config filtering

root_causes.hypothesis: Plugins execute in-process with full Node.js/Bun API access — no VM sandbox or Worker isolation
evidence: packages/opencode/src/plugin/loader.ts:135 — dynamic import(row.entry) executes plugin code in-process
.build/rig-relay/plugin_security_surface.v1.json:110 — 'No Worker thread isolation, no vm.Script, no ShadowRealm'
packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts:167 — plugin.server(input, options) called directly in host process

fixes.description: Add new CapabilityId values for SDK, shell, and network egress access
fixes.before: Existing 14 CapabilityId values (FilesystemRead through ConfigWrite). No shell or SDK-specific capabilities.
fixes.after: Add 3 new capability values: ShellExecute, SdkAccess, NetworkFetch. Update FALLBACK_MANIFEST to include backwards-compatible defaults (SdkAccess, ShellExecute, NetworkFetch) alongside existing tool.register.
risks: Adding new CapabilityId values does not break existing code (they are additive to a const object)
FALLBACK_MANIFEST expansion changes behavior: external plugins gain more default capabilities. This is intentional for backwards compat.
validation: bun typecheck in packages/opencode — verify no type errors from new CapabilityId values
Existing plugin trigger test should still pass
Manual audit: grep for CapabilityId values to ensure no hardcoded exhaustiveness checks

fixes.description: Create SDK namespace → CapabilityId mapping table
fixes.before: No mapping exists; HookDispatchGuard uses HOOK_CAPABILITY_MAP for hook names only.
fixes.after: New file with SDK_CAPABILITY_MAP: Record<string, CapabilityId> mapping 20 SDK namespaces (global, project, pty, config, tool, instance, path, vcs, session, command, provider, find, file, app, mcp, lsp, formatter, tui, auth, event) to their required capabilities. Also includes SHELL_DENYLIST_COMMANDS: Set<string> for dangerous shell patterns blocked even with shell.execute.

tests_to_run: New unit test: verify every SDK namespace has a mapping entry

risks: If a new SDK namespace is added in codegen, it won't appear in this map — consider runtime logging for unknown namespaces
Overly restrictive mapping could break built-in auth plugins that use auth namespace extensively
validation: Cross-reference all 20 properties of OpencodeClient class against SDK_CAPABILITY_MAP keys
Count should be exactly 20 entries matching the OpencodeClient property count

fixes.description: Create capability-gated Proxy wrappers (SDK client, shell $, fetch, env)
fixes.before: No proxy exists. Plugins receive raw OpencodeClient, raw Bun.$, raw process.env, raw fetch().
fixes.after:
New file (~180 lines) containing 4 functions:
- makeScopedClient(client, allowedCaps): Proxy<OpencodeClient> — intercepts property access on SDK namespaces; checks SDK_CAPABILITY_MAP; returns a no-op stub (logs warning) for denied namespaces
- makeScopedShell(shell, allowedCaps): Proxy<BunShell> — wraps tagged template call; checks shell.execute capability; also checks SHELL_DENYLIST_COMMANDS
- makeScopedFetch(allowedCaps): typeof fetch — checks network.fetch capability before allowing outbound requests; logs blocked requests
- makeFilteredEnv(allowedCaps): Record<string, string|undefined> — filters secrets (keys matching hasSecretKey pattern) unless secrets.access granted
- makePluginInputProxy(input, pluginId, registry): PluginInput — convenience that creates all 4 proxies for a plugin's input
Uses a synchronous capability cache (Map<string, Set<string>>) populated at registration time alongside the Effect Ref registry.

tests_to_run: New: packages/opencode/test/plugin/capability-proxy.test.ts — unit tests for each proxy function

risks: Proxy type erasure: TypeScript can't fully infer Proxy return types — need explicit type assertion
Proxy overhead: every SDK property access goes through Proxy.get trap (~microseconds, acceptable)
Prototype chain bypass: plugins could access original client via Object.getPrototypeOf(proxyClient) — mitigated by wrapping class methods not just property access
BunShell is a tagged template function AND an object with methods — Proxy must handle both call and get traps

Synchronous capability cache diverging from Effect Ref: if cache update fails but Ref succeeds (or vice versa), capability state is inconsistent. Mitigation: populate cache from Ref.get() at proxy construction time, not dual-write
built-in plugins (trust: "built-in") bypass all checks per checkCapability logic — intentional, no change needed
validation: Unit: create mock OpencodeClient, wrap with proxy for external plugin (FALLBACK_MANIFEST caps), verify tool namespace accessible, session namespace denied
Unit: wrap BunShell proxy, call $`echo test` with shell.execute → allowed, without → denied
Unit: scoped fetch blocks outbound requests without network.fetch
Unit: filtered env strips SECRET_KEY but keeps PATH with secrets.access=false
Integration: full plugin load → trigger hook → verify SDK calls are gated
Type check: bun typecheck in packages/opencode must pass

fixes.description: Add fetch and env to PluginInput type and construct scoped input per-plugin

before.packages/plugin/src/index.ts: PluginInput has: client, project, directory, worktree, experimental_workspace, serverUrl, $. No fetch or env field.
before.packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts: Single PluginInput constructed at line 226. applyPlugin passes raw input to plugin.server(). External plugins registered once with makeFallbackState('external').

after.packages/plugin/src/index.ts: PluginInput adds: fetch: typeof fetch, env: Record<string, string | undefined>. Both optional for backwards compat.
after.packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts: In applyPlugin (or surrounding loop), wrap input with makePluginInputProxy() per-plugin before passing to plugin.server(). The synchronous capability cache is populated from the registry at wrapping time. Raw client construction stays for built-in plugins (trust: built-in → all capabilities).

tests_to_run: bun typecheck — verify no type errors from PluginInput change
Full test suite to catch any PluginInput destructuring breakage

risks: PluginInput type change is additive (new optional fields) — existing plugins that destructure PluginInput won't break

Raw process.env/fetch() globals are still accessible in-process — plugins can bypass the sanctioned API. This is documented as a known limitation; true process isolation requires Worker threads (deferred to Phase 3 per rl-005 plan)

applyPlugin is an async function; capability registry is Effect-based. The synchronous cache must be read before calling applyPlugin. Solution: pre-populate cache entries during capabilityRegistry.register() calls (which already happen before applyPlugin)
validation: Write a test plugin that accesses input.fetch and input.env — verify they exist and are functional
Load a plugin without a manifest — verify FALLBACK_MANIFEST caps are applied via proxy
Load a built-in plugin — verify it gets full access (trust: built-in)

fixes.before: No consent flow. Plugins are installed and loaded without user awareness of capabilities.
fixes.after:
New file (~80 lines) with consent flow design:
- ConsentGate type: { pluginId, manifest, granted, timestamp }
- ConsentStore: persists consent decisions to disk (JSON file in project .opencode/ dir)
- checkConsent(pluginId, manifest): Effect<boolean> — checks if user has granted consent for requested capabilities
- requestConsent(pluginId, manifest): Effect<boolean> — triggers UI consent prompt (via bus event) and waits for user response
- On first load of an external plugin with capabilities beyond FALLBACK_MANIFEST, block loading until consent is granted
- For plugins using only FALLBACK_MANIFEST capabilities, auto-consent with log warning
Integration point: after capabilityRegistry.register() in the init flow, call checkConsent/requestConsent before proceeding to load the plugin.
risks: Consent flow requires user interaction (blocking) — must not block startup for already-consented plugins
Consent store file format must be forwards-compatible (version field)
UI consent prompt requires bus event integration — ensure event is handled even if no UI is attached (auto-deny in headless mode)
validation: Load a new plugin → verify consent prompt event is published on bus
Load an already-consented plugin → verify no prompt, loads immediately
Headless mode → verify auto-deny for new capabilities

rollback_plan: Revert each fix in reverse order (E→A). Fixes A-C are additive (new files + optional exports), fix-D adds optional fields to PluginInput (backwards compatible). Full rollback: revert plugin/index.ts, delete new capability files, remove PluginInput fields. No data migration needed (consent store is append-only).

ShellExecute.description: Ability to execute shell commands via the $ BunShell tagged template
ShellExecute.rationale: Backwards compatibility: existing plugins that use $ continue to work

SdkAccess.description: Coarse-grained ability to access the OpencodeClient SDK. Without this, the SDK client proxy returns no-op stubs for all namespaces
SdkAccess.rationale: Backwards compatibility: existing plugins that use any SDK method continue to work. Future: split into fine-grained per-namespace capabilities

NetworkFetch.description: Ability to make outbound HTTP requests via fetch(). Distinct from network.request which gates internal SDK HTTP calls
NetworkFetch.rationale: Backwards compatibility: existing plugins using fetch() through the sanctioned API continue to work

after.note: Secrets.access, tool.execute, filesystem.write, config.write remain excluded. Existing external plugins using only FALLBACK_MANIFEST capabilities continue working. Plugins needing excluded capabilities must declare a manifest.

key_architectural_decisions: Synchronous capability cache (Map<string, Set<CapabilityId>>) alongside Effect Ref — avoids Proxy trap async problem
Proxy-based SDK client gating — intercepts namespace access at get trap level
FALLBACK_MANIFEST expansion for backwards compatibility — existing plugins get generous defaults
Sanctioned fetch()/env on PluginInput rather than true global interception — process isolation deferred
Consent flow as bus event (non-blocking at startup for already-consented plugins)
deferred_to_future_waves: Process isolation (Worker threads / ShadowRealm) — true sandboxing for process.env/fetch() globals
Runtime capability usage auditing (track which plugins use which capabilities)
Manifest signing and verification (npm provenance / Sigstore)
Network egress allowlisting per origin (network_origins in CapabilityManifest)
